title: Fix linkedin issues
author: Srabon888
content: Read carefully to being prevent linkedin locked or suspende I recommend logging into LinkedIn using a reliable proxy, preferably a 5G mobile proxy like T-Mobile or AT&T. The choice of proxy is crucial for LinkedIn, and using the right one can make your LinkedIn experience smoother. For example, I suggest using an AT&T proxy. Also, make sure to use a good browser; you can opt for Firefox or an Antidetect browser. However, not all Antidetect browsers are effective, as LinkedIn can detect some of them. Once you have the right proxy and browser, log in to LinkedIn and take it slow. Avoid spamming early on; instead, give it one or two days before logging out. After that, log back in, and it's essential to log in with your LinkedIn email address. Keep an eye on any emails from LinkedIn, especially those regarding suspicious login activity. Always open these emails as LinkedIn tracks your interaction with them. Failing to open these emails might trigger suspicions of unauthorized account use, potentially leading to an account suspension. Additionally, refrain from excessive likes, comments, or connection requests. Instead, spend time scrolling, reading, and building a trust score before engaging in more active interactions. Following these steps should help prevent your account from being locked or suspended. Let me know if you have any questions or need further assistance!

title: Why do most high-achievers avoid entrepreneurship?
author: megakim
content: I’ve known a lot of people way smarter than me like engineers, analysts, strategy people, who never even consider starting their own thing. Supposedly, only about 0.1% of highly talented people go the entrepreneurship route. I’m no genius myself, but I do run a SaaS, and it’s wild to me how much brainpower sits on the sidelines. After meeting hundreds of high-potential people in corporate and tech, I think it’s less about money or ideas and more about comfort. Smart people often build stable, well-paid careers, and the thought of ditching that certainty for the chaos of entrepreneurship is just not appealing. Also, there’s the curse of seeing too many risks. Being able to see every possible way something could go wrong can paralyze you before you ever start. When I started my business SocLeads, there were at least ten voices in my head telling me why it would fail and none telling me to just try it and learn. Most people don’t need more brains or better ideas, they need permission to try, get things wrong, and not have it wreck their self-image. I really believe talent is everywhere, but momentum is rare. Sometimes average people win just because they’re willing to send that first cold email, launch an early version, or risk looking dumb.

title: Manufactured UGC on my own forum site to rank
author: ryanster1999
content: Hey guys, I’m in the casino affiliate niche and run an established community along with a Discourse forum. I’ve been experimenting by creating threads and replies in my own forum copying long tail queries from Ahrefs and have seen some interesting results. For example, I’ll look up questions in Ahrefs and find queries like “Is Zula Casino instant payout?” Then I’ll create that exact question as a thread on my forum. Later I’ll reply from another account with something like, “No, it’s not instant. It usually takes around 3 to 5 days. If you’re looking for casinos with instant payouts you can check out this list here: [link].” Doing this, I’ve managed to rank for about half a dozen long tail questions already. I should also mention that the forum does have real engagement, so these test threads are mixed in with organic discussions from actual members. My question is whether this is safe to scale up, or if Google would eventually figure out that I’m seeding some of these discussions myself and tank the whole site. For example, if I started creating a lot more of these Q&A style threads targeting specific long tail searches, then replying and subtlety linking in my pillar posts. Is that a good idea or could it backfire from an SEO standpoint? Curious if anyone here has experience with this. Thanks in advance. Cheers!!
